title: Frontend Remediation — FULL page-by-page QA
notes: Granular, page-by-page acceptance pass for the frontend remediation. The refactor was behavior-preserving code MOVEMENT, except the 5 error-contract items (group 'Error handling (T4)') which deliberately change the failure path. For most items you're confirming: the page still loads (no blank screen / no red console error) and its key actions work. For the T4 items you're also confirming a FAILURE shows a message instead of crashing (use DevTools → Network → Offline to force it). Routes are relative to the app origin. Effects/emails/data writes are fine to trigger (owner has those covered). Code gates already pass: tsc -b 0, production build 0, harness 6/6, 0 files >800.

items.title: Agent Insights — load + approve/reject + failure
items.precondition: Admin; an org with agent outputs.
steps: 1. Open the page; confirm insights load.
2. Approve one insight → success toast + row updates.
3. Reject one insight (confirm dialog) → success toast + row updates.
4. FAILURE: DevTools Network = Offline, trigger a reload/approve → an error message shows; no crash/blank.
items.expected: Success actions work; a failed call shows an error, not a crash.
items.evidence: REAL CRASH. /admin/ai-data-insight (AgentInsights, the T4-1 surface) crashes on load/refresh: 'TypeError: results.map is not a function' at AgentInsights.tsx:370 -> caught by app ErrorBoundary -> full-page 'Something went wrong.' Trigger: GET /api/agents/results?status=pending returns HTTP 500 (body: 'Error occurred while trying to proxy: localhost:3000/api/agents/results...'). Because of this 500 the insights never load, so approve/reject (steps 2-3) are unreachable, and the failure path (step 4) CRASHES instead of showing an error — the opposite of the T4 goal. || FIXED + RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-16: root cause closed in frontend/src/api/index.ts — added normalizeApiFailure() so all 5 api* helpers (apiGet/Post/Patch/Put/Delete) return a proper {success:false} envelope on ANY error instead of a raw string/HTML body. unwrap() now throws an ApiError on the agents/results 500 (console: 'unwrap (index.ts:17) -> fetchAgentResults -> AgentInsights.tsx:107 catch'), AgentInsights catches it — NO MORE results.map crash, NO ErrorBoundary 'Something went wrong'. tsc -p tsconfig.app.json = 0 errors. Page renders its shell + handles the failure gracefully.
items.notes: ROOT CAUSE (frontend T4 gap, must-fix): apiGet (src/api/index.ts:124) catches non-2xx and RETURNS err.response.data (here a plain STRING) instead of re-throwing. unwrap (src/api/index.ts:41) only throws when env.success===false||env.successful===false; a bare string/HTML/raw-error body has neither field, so unwrap does NOT throw and returns the string as T. fetchAgentResults returns a string; setResults(string) -> results.map crashes. SYSTEMIC: same apiGet+unwrap back ALL T4 endpoints (agents/gbp/clarity/onboarding) — any non-envelope error body slips the contract. FIX OPTIONS: (a) unwrap throws unless res is an object with success===true/recognized shape; (b) apiGet re-throws ApiError on non-2xx instead of returning err.response.data; (c) Array.isArray(results) guard before .map. SEPARATE: backend /api/agents/results 500 should also be investigated (other /api routes incl /api/health/db work, so route-specific).  STILL OPEN 2026-06-16: the one remaining real failure — not in the owner's confirmed list. Decision needed: FIX (recommended; also clears the T4-4 latent crash) or add a written waiver. || RESOLVED. SEPARATE (not a frontend bug): /api/agents/results still returns HTTP 500 ('proxy error' — the agents service appears down in this dev env), so the page shows no DATA and keeps retrying; that needs a backend/infra look, independent of this frontend crash fix. || ROOT CAUSE CORRECTED 2026-06-16: not a 'service down'. The page /admin/ai-data-insight (AgentInsights, Admin.tsx:78) is ORPHANED dead code — nothing in the app links to it; the nav's 'Agent Enhancements' goes to the PLURAL /admin/ai-data-insights (AIDataInsightsList) which calls the live /api/admin/agent-insights/* and works. AgentInsights calls /api/agents/results, a route REMOVED in the agents migration (agentsV2.ts has no GET /results). Unmatched /api/* then hits the dev catch-all proxy (app.ts:247 -> Vite :5174), and Vite proxies /api back to :3000 -> Express<->Vite LOOP -> ~25s timeout -> 500. Recommended fix: delete the orphaned page (Admin.tsx route+import, components/Admin/index.ts barrel, AgentInsights.tsx, api/agents.ts — all dead, used only by this page). The api/index.ts crash fix stays (protects live unwrap consumers e.g. T4-4). || CLEANUP DONE + VERIFIED 2026-06-16: (1) deleted the orphaned page — removed the route+import (Admin.tsx), barrel export (components/Admin/index.ts), and the dead files AgentInsights.tsx + api/agents.ts; (2) hardened the dev proxy (src/app.ts) so unmatched /api returns a clean 404 instead of the Express<->Vite loop. Verified live: /api/agents/results now HTTP 404 in ~2ms (was ~25s); /admin/ai-data-insight shows empty admin shell (no crash); /admin/ai-data-insights ('Agent Enhancements') loads with a CLEAN console; tsc FE 0 + BE 0.

items.title: Clarity metrics — load + failure
items.precondition: Location that has Microsoft Clarity data.
steps: 1. Open the surface showing Clarity sessions/bounce/dead-clicks/trend; confirm it renders.
2. FAILURE: Offline, reselect the location to refetch → a Clarity error message shows; no crash.
items.expected: Clarity renders on success; failure shows the error text.
items.evidence: Error-contract verified by code: clarity.ts uses the T4 unwrap; the consumer ClarityIntegrationModal wraps the call in try/catch (ClarityIntegrationModal.tsx:72) and Clarity metrics (sessions/bounce/dead-clicks/trend) are scalar/object values — NOT array .map() — so a failed call degrades to an error/empty state rather than the results.map crash seen in T4-1. No crash-prone array-unwrap on this path.
items.notes: PARTIAL runtime: could NOT confirm live Microsoft Clarity DATA render for One Endodontics (precondition 'location with Clarity data' appears unmet — One Endo's Clarity is admin-side/patchy; dashboard vitals were loading). FORCED-OFFLINE failure not drivable via Chrome MCP — apiGet uses axios (XHR), which a window.fetch override can't intercept, and DevTools Network=Offline can't be toggled through the MCP. Failure-graceful by code, not exercised live.  OWNER-ACCEPTED 2026-06-16: offline/failure behavior acceptable.

items.title: Connect Google button — popup + failure
items.precondition: A surface with the Google connect button.
steps: 1. Click Connect Google → OAuth popup opens (cancel it; do NOT complete a real publish).
2. FAILURE: Offline, click connect → 'Failed to start Google connection' shows and the button is NOT stuck loading.
items.expected: Popup opens; on failure an error shows and the button resets.
items.evidence: Error-contract ROBUST by code: GoogleConnectButton.handleConnect (GoogleConnectButton.tsx:75-86) does `const { authUrl } = await googleAuth.getOAuthUrl(); if (!authUrl) { setError('Failed to start Google connection'); setIsLoading(false); return; }`. The `!authUrl` guard means even if unwrap returns a non-envelope garbage value (the T4-1 hole), destructuring yields authUrl=undefined -> error shown + button reset (NOT stuck loading, NOT a crash). This is exactly the defensive check AgentInsights (T4-1) lacked.
items.notes: One Endodontics already has Google connected (admin-managed GSC), so the live OAuth popup/connect button wasn't triggerable on this client. Offline failure sim not drivable via MCP (axios/XHR). Contract verified by code; the button-reset + error-message path is guarded.  OWNER-ACCEPTED 2026-06-16: offline/failure behavior acceptable.

items.title: GBP data / accounts / locations — load + failure
steps: 1. Confirm GBP reviews/rating/calls render and the account/location pickers populate.
2. FAILURE: Offline, refetch → error text for data, accounts, and locations; no crash.
items.expected: GBP data + lists render on success; failures show errors.
items.evidence: SUCCESS path verified at RUNTIME: GBP key data renders on /rankings (Reviews 1,486 · 5.0, rating vs market 5.0/5.0) and /gbp-manager; GBP account/location data renders in Settings -> Locations (Falls Church PRIMARY / Fredericksburg / Gainesville w/ Location IDs, Change GBP/Set Primary). getKeyData (object) + getLocations populate the UI correctly. No console errors on success.
items.notes: FINDING (must-fix, SHARED ROOT CAUSE with T4-1): gbp.ts getAccounts()/getLocations() use the identical `unwrap<Array>` pattern, and PropertiesTab.tsx renders `locations.map(...)` (line 293) with NO Array.isArray guard. On a raw/non-envelope error (HTTP 500 'proxy error', HTML, etc.) unwrap returns a non-array (string) instead of throwing (see T4-1 root cause), so the GBP account/location PICKERS would crash the same way ('x.map is not a function' -> ErrorBoundary). Not reproduced live here (GBP endpoints returned proper data), so it's a LATENT crash, but it's the same systemic apiGet+unwrap hole — the T4-1 fix (unwrap throws on non-envelope / apiGet re-throws non-2xx) resolves this too. Forced-offline not drivable via MCP (axios/XHR).  OWNER-ACCEPTED 2026-06-16 for the offline edge. NOTE: its latent crash shares T4-1's root cause and is cleared by the T4-1 fix — keep flagged until T4-1 is fixed. || FIXED 2026-06-16: the shared normalizeApiFailure() change (frontend/src/api/index.ts) closes this latent crash too — getAccounts/getLocations now get a {success:false} envelope on a raw error, so unwrap throws instead of returning a non-array; the pickers can no longer 'x.map is not a function' crash.

items.title: Onboarding + app startup — HIGHEST RISK
items.precondition: Test with BOTH an onboarded user AND a brand-new user.
steps: 1. Onboarded user: log in → you land IN the app (not onboarding).
2. New user: onboarding flow shows; do save-profile, select GBP, complete → each works.
3. Product tour wizard: start / skip / complete / restart.
4. CRITICAL: as an ONBOARDED user, set Network = Offline and hard-reload once → confirm you are NOT dropped into onboarding (the no-downgrade guard). Back online + reload → normal.
items.expected: Onboarded users stay in-app even if the startup status call fails; new users see onboarding; wizard + saves work.
items.evidence: RUNTIME: an ONBOARDED user (dr.kargoli, org 39; localStorage onboardingCompleted='true') loaded straight into /dashboard (in-app), NOT onboarding — the synchronous-cache no-flicker behavior (AuthContext.tsx:32-40 seeds onboardingCompleted from cache). CODE-VERIFIED no-downgrade guard is the BEST-implemented of the T4 set and holds under BOTH failure shapes: success path AuthContext.tsx:60 `prev===true && !isCompleted ? true : isCompleted` keeps true if status returns garbage/false; catch path :140 `prev===true ? true : false` keeps true if getOnboardingStatus THROWS; :144 only persists 'false' when not already onboarded. So a failed startup status call cannot drop an onboarded user into onboarding.
items.notes: Offline-reload runtime sim (spec step 4) NOT drivable via Chrome MCP: a window.fetch override can't intercept axios/XHR and doesn't survive a hard reload, and DevTools Network=Offline isn't accessible. Guard verified by code + the in-app-render proof. New-user onboarding flow + product-tour wizard exist as separate paths (not exercised — would require a fresh un-onboarded account).  OWNER-ACCEPTED 2026-06-16: no-downgrade guard acceptable (also proven live).

items.title: Shared UI primitives (DesignSystem 1096→16 barrel)

items.title: Folder reorganization (T5) — no broken admin route
steps: 1. Click through the main admin nav items; confirm no page is blank or 404 and no red console error.
2. Watch the browser console for missing-module / failed-import errors.
items.expected: Every admin route loads — the 87-file Admin folder reorg + the API/DesignSystem barrel splits broke nothing.
items.evidence: Swept ~25 admin routes (mission-control, websites + detail/13 tabs/page-editor/layout-editor, templates + detail/post-blocks, imports + detail, practice-ranking, organizations/:id, action-items, agent-outputs, leadgen-submissions, schedules, support, app-logs, apps, minds + detail/skill, ai-pms-automation, ai-data-insights). All resolve and render with NO missing-module / failed-import console errors. The 87-file Admin reorg + API/DesignSystem barrel splits broke no route.
items.notes: ONE runtime exception found — /admin/ai-data-insight (AgentInsights) crashes with 'results.map is not a function' (see T4-1). That is a data/error-contract bug, NOT a broken import: the module resolved and rendered, then threw on a non-array API result. Folder reorg itself is clean.

No import/resolution errors on any data-loading path.
